vulnerability.title: Configure Time Service Maxpoll Interval
vulnerability.description: The maxpoll should be configured to in /etc/ntp.conf or /etc/chrony.conf (or /etc/chrony.d/) to continuously poll time servers. To configure maxpoll in /etc/ntp.conf or /etc/chrony.conf (or /etc/chrony.d/) add the following after each server, pool or peer entry: maxpoll to server directives. If using chrony, any pool directives should be configured too.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( rpm --quiet -q chrony || rpm --quiet -q ntp ); }; then var_time_service_set_maxpoll='' pof="/usr/sbin/pidof" CONFIG_FILES="/etc/ntp.conf" $pof ntpd || { CHRONY_D_PATH=/etc/chrony.d/ mapfile -t CONFIG_FILES < <(find ${CHRONY_D_PATH}.* -type f -name '*.conf') CONFIG_FILES+=(/etc/chrony.conf) } # get list of ntp files for config_file in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}" ; do # Set maxpoll values to var_time_service_set_maxpoll sed -i "s/^\(\(server\|pool\|peer\).*maxpoll\) [0-9,-][0-9]*\(.*\)$/\1 $var_time_service_set_maxpoll \3/" "$config_file" done for config_file in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}" ; do # Add maxpoll to server, pool or peer entries without maxpoll grep "^\(server\|pool\|peer\)" "$config_file" | grep -v maxpo

triage_decision.reason: This finding involves configuring the maxpoll interval for time synchronization services (NTP or chrony). The remediation script provided is a standard configuration change that adds or modifies the maxpoll directive in the appropriate configuration files. This type of change is well-tested and follows standard STIG hardening patterns. It does not affect authentication, authorization, networking configuration, or filesystems in a way that would cause system breakage or lockout.

commands_executed: echo "No remediation needed - maxpoll 16 already configured on all pool directives in /etc/chrony.conf"

execution_details.stdout:
chrony-4.6.1-2.el9.x86_64
package ntp is not installed

# Use public servers from the pool.ntp.org project.
# Please consider joining the pool (https://www.pool.ntp.org/join.html).

# Record the rate at which the system clock gains/losses time.
driftfile /var/lib/chrony/drift

# Allow the system clock to be stepped in the first three updates
# if its offset is larger than 1 second.
makestep 1.0 3

# Enable kernel synchronization of the real-time clock (RTC).
rtcsync

# Specify directory for log files.
logdir /var/lib/chrony

# Select which information is logged.
#log measurements statistics tracking

# Use NTP servers from Rocky Linux pool.
pool 0.rocky.pool.ntp.org iburst maxpoll 16
pool 1.rocky.pool.ntp.org iburst maxpoll 16
pool 2.rocky.pool.ntp.org iburst maxpoll 16

# Set port to 0 (client-only mode)
port 0

# Set cmdport to 0 (no command port)
cmdport 0
